description: Issue #19: Plan contract for scaffold planning
steps: A public `Plan` domain model exists for classified entries, tree nodes, merge requirements, warnings, and structured causes.
The contract supports `create`, `modify`, `unchanged`, and `needsMergeStrategy` classifications.
The contract is deterministic and narrow enough for the first planning slice described in the parent PRD.
`PlanService.build` accepts a resolved `Blueprint` plus `repoRoot` and returns a `Plan`.
Empty-directory planning projects `root-bootstrap` outputs into deterministic flat entries and tree nodes.
The slice does not write files to disk and only prepares the virtual plan output.

description: Issue #21: Blueprint-driven repo snapshot loading for planning
steps: A `RepoSnapshotLoader` service exists and is driven by the confirmed blueprint.
Snapshot loading is selective for file content and supports broader path or directory existence checks where needed.
The service boundary is ready for existing-repo plan classification without forcing planning to model the whole repository.

description: Issue #22: Plan package public exports from blueprint composition
steps: Planning reads blueprint package composition when projecting package export-related outputs.
The first-slice `packages/domain` composition can drive the `./Api` export shape in the plan.
The slice stays aligned with the current blueprint contract even though flat blueprint intents are not emitted yet.

description: Issue #23: Plan implied packages/domain outputs from blueprint dependencies
steps: Planning includes implied canonical `packages/domain` outputs when required by the confirmed blueprint.
The implied package appears in the nested tree and flat entries with deterministic ordering.
Planned paths retain structured provenance showing why the implied package exists.

description: Issue #24: Plan server target paths from the current blueprint graph
steps: Planning consumes the current blueprint graph for a selected `server` target without re-running blueprint resolution.
The server target produces projected paths in the flat plan entries and nested tree.
Planned server paths retain structured provenance back to the originating blueprint target or module.

description: Issue #25: Bootstrap file classification in existing repositories
steps: Existing-repo bootstrap planning compares projected files with repo snapshot content.
Bootstrap paths are classified as `create`, `modify`, or `unchanged` for authoritative full-file outputs.
The resulting plan remains deterministic and uses the public `Plan` contract established by the parent slices.

description: Issue #26: Safe package export merges in planning
steps: Planning supports safe additive export updates for recognized `package.json` shapes.
Export additions for the first-slice package flow can be projected through recognized structured merges.
Unrecognized export merge cases are deferred to merge-requirement handling rather than opaque JSON patching.

description: Issue #27: Safe package dependency merges in planning
steps: Planning supports safe additive dependency updates for recognized `package.json` shapes.
Planned dependency additions retain section information such as `dependencies` or `devDependencies`.
Unrecognized dependency merge cases are left for merge-requirement handling rather than arbitrary patching.

description: Issue #28: Simple barrel export merges in planning
steps: Planning recognizes simple barrel file shapes such as `src/index.ts`.
Additive barrel exports can be planned safely and surfaced as structured plan changes.
Unsupported barrel shapes are left to merge-requirement handling rather than arbitrary text patching.

description: Issue #29: Conservative tsconfig handling in planning
steps: Planning handles known `tsconfig.json` files conservatively as create, exact-match unchanged, or `needsMergeStrategy`.
The slice does not attempt broad `tsconfig.json` merge behavior.
Resulting plan entries remain deterministic and compatible with the public `Plan` contract.

description: Issue #30: Safe package script merges in planning
steps: Planning supports safe additive script updates for recognized `package.json` shapes.
Script additions appear as `modify` plan entries when merged into supported files.
Unrecognized script merge cases are deferred to merge-requirement handling rather than patched arbitrarily.

description: Issue #31: End-to-end planning fixtures and ordering tests
steps: End-to-end planning tests use real temporary directories rather than heavy filesystem mocking.
Coverage includes empty bootstrap, existing repo reconciliation, structured merge cases, and ambiguity continuation.
Tests assert deterministic ordering, tree projection, and structured provenance through public plan outputs.

description: Issue #32: Merge requirements and needsMergeStrategy continuation
steps: Ambiguous or unsupported merge cases are surfaced as `needsMergeStrategy` entries.
Structured merge requirements are attached to the plan with path-level attribution.
Planning continues for unaffected paths when one or more paths require merge strategy decisions.
